titleEn: Poultry WorldHatchTech's 3-phase incubation aims to reshape hatchery managementJul 14Interview
titleZh: HatchTech的三阶段孵化概念：重塑孵化场管理
summaryZh: HatchTech在VIV Europe上推出三阶段孵化概念，挑战传统两阶段孵化标准。该方案结合AI照蛋系统SetGate（第9天精准区分受精蛋）、SetCare（缓慢升温降低死亡率）和HatchCare（出壳后提供饲料、水和光照），可提升孵化容量15%，提高雏鸡均匀度和存活率。文章通过CEO和资深科学家的访谈，详细阐述了技术原理、实施路径及对行业效率的潜在影响。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了AI照蛋、精准温控和早期饲喂技术在禽业孵化环节的集成应用，为智慧畜牧从业者提供了从传统两阶段向三阶段升级的可行路径，具有较高的技术参考和效率提升价值。
contentFull: HatchTech's 3-phase incubation aims to reshape hatchery managementIncubation specialist HatchTech unveiled its new 3-phase incubation concept at VIV Europe in June. The team behind the introduction of feed and water in the hatcher (HatchCare) and the concept of 24-day incubation instead of 21 days (SetCare) says its latest innovation redefines the incubation process. Poultry World spoke to CEO Tjitze Meter and senior scientist Dr Jan Wijnen to find out what is behind the new approach.Fabian BrockötterEditor in Chief, Poultry WorldJul 14InterviewHatchTech CEO Tjitze Meter (l) founded the company in 1996 after a career as hatchery advisor. What started in a shed with improving incubator, grew into a worldwide business with facilities in multiple countries. His senior scientist Dr Jan Wijnen completed a bachelor’s programme in Animal Husbandry and holds a master’s degree in animal sciences at Wageningen University. He joined HatchTech in 2016. Photo: Herbert Wiggerman QWell, what are you up to?"Getting as close as possible to the biological process, learning from the past and introducing the latest technology. That philosophy has led us to many innovations, including the new 3-phase incubation concept. This innovative approach challenges the long-standing 2-phase standard used across the industry, resulting in higher output and greater efficiency”, says Tjitze Meter.Researcher Dr Jan Wijnen continues: “3-phase incubation can be implemented in existing hatcheries with standard machines. The ideal setup to improve hatchery results requires dedicated equipment.”QCan you explain the process?"Phase 1 starts with a 9-day incubation period. After 9 days, eggs are assessed using SetGate, an in-house-de veloped candling system. This AI-driven technology enables early, highly accurate distinction between fertile eggs and in fertile or non-viable ones. This greatly reduces potential bacterial loads,” according to Wijnen.Meter continues: “Candling at day 9 makes candling at day 18 redundant. After early candling, the incubation process continues in phase 2 at traditional, high-capacity setters, with up to a 15% increase in capacity, depending on the setters' cooling capacity. In phase 3, at day 18, the eggs are transferred directly to hatchers without candling. Phase 1 can be further optimised using SetCare instead of a standard setter. In SetCare, the eggs are gradually warmed after storage at a controlled rate of 0.1F per hour. This slow warming at the beginning of cell replication of an embryo (from 85F to 100F) results in significantly lower mortality and stronger, more uniform chicks."Wijnen continues: “In phase 3, research shows that poultry performance can be improved by using HatchCare, providing chicks with feed, water and light directly after hatching.”QI see a combination of 3 quite innovative systems and a concept that challenges traditional hatchery practices. Have you lost your mind?"In the first 10-15 years of our company’s existence, many in the industry did indeed think that. But, 3 decades later, I believe that perspective has evolved, and I am confident our latest concept will contribute to that," Meter laughs. "Our industry is quite conservative, and many developments in technology have been instigated by cost saving. Many integrators see the hatchery as a cost centre rather than the foundation of current and future performance and returns for the entire production chain. In our philosophy, we want to get the most out of the hatchery, start with the best chicks, hatched under the most optimal circumstances."Wijnen continues: "At HatchTech, everything starts with research, often with at least 4-5 years of getting to the bottom of embryo biology, and how they actually react to adaptations in incubation conditions. In the early days of Hatchtech, embryo temperature in relation to chick length and quality received a lot of focus, as did the discrepancy between the temperature in the hatcher and that on the hatcher's display. Since 2004, we have had multiple PhD projects, all focused on building a sound foundation and deeper knowledge, taking that as a starting point for further optimising machines and processes. Knowledge we openly share by the way, potentially profiting the whole industry."QLet's zoom in. How did you get to 3-phase incubation?"The biggest shift our concept brings is the timing of candling from day 18 to day 9. That seems odd by current standards, but the more veteran people in our industry will surely remember early candling," Meter adds. He continues: "In the old days, we were pulling trays from the setter to manually candle them at around day 7. Clear eggs would then be sold to bakeries. A change in regulations prohibited this practice, and together with the onset of further automation, we all moved to candling at day 18. Since we moved from manual nest boxes to automated nests on parent stock farms, bacterial load on hatching eggs increased. It is well known that one of a setter’s core strengths is cell reproduction. Obviously, in the egg and embryo, but also all unwanted bacteria will replicate. In essence, you want to get rid of bad bacteria in bad eggs as early as possible to prevent risk-filled bacterial load in your incubators."QWhat was needed to make 3-phase incubation possible?"Even though we already knew the benefits of early candling, we lacked the machine to do this. That changed when our sister company Seleggt developed and optimised its in-ovo sexing technology for layers. For that process, sexing needs to be done before day 13 of incubation, as before that day, there is no pain sensation in the embryo, so male layers can be removed without animal welfare implications. You don’t want to waste sexing costs by evaluating an infertile or bad egg. This demanded a machine that could select these eggs early in the incubation process. That's where our SetGate machine came in," Meter explains.Wijnen continues: "The candling machine was developed, built and extensively tested in-house for Seleggt, but with other applications in the back of our mind. We must take into account that the embryo in the egg at day 9 is very different from the embryo at day 18. We learned, for instance, that the maximum impact (acceleration/deceleration) must remain below 1G in young embryos to prevent damage. And we tested different light spectra, cameras and artificial intelligence settings to get the desired outcome. We are now extremely close to 100% accuracy in detecting clear and bad eggs. With the help of AI, we can even detect mid-term mortality before the embryo actually dies. A nice example was when we, as humans, were outperformed by artificial intelligence."QHow do you expect the industry to respond to 3-phase incubation?Meter: "We have been talking about this for years internally, and we do think this is the moment for getting the word out there. We are in a market of both early adopters and conservative minds, but everyone wants to move ahead. Developments and investments in hatcheries are made for the long run, for the next generation, so to speak. For us as a company, we need to think about this future, which means we can never be too far ahead."
translationZh:
**那么，你们在做什么？**

“尽可能贴近生物过程，从过去中学习，并引入最新技术。这一理念引领我们实现了多项创新，包括全新的三阶段孵化概念。这种创新方法挑战了行业内长期使用的两阶段标准，带来了更高的产出和效率。”Tjitze Meter说道。

研究员Jan Wijnen博士继续说道：“三阶段孵化可以在现有孵化场中使用标准机器实施。但要获得理想的孵化结果改进，需要专用设备。”

**你能解释一下这个过程吗？**

“第一阶段从9天的孵化期开始。9天后，使用内部开发的照蛋系统SetGate对鸡蛋进行评估。这项AI驱动技术能够早期、高精度地区分受精蛋与未受精或不可存活蛋。这大大降低了潜在的细菌负荷。”Wijnen说。

Meter继续说道：“在第9天进行照蛋，使得第18天的照蛋变得多余。早期照蛋后，孵化过程在第二阶段继续，使用传统的高容量孵化器，容量最多可增加15%，具体取决于孵化器的冷却能力。在第三阶段，即第18天，鸡蛋直接转移到出雏器，无需照蛋。第一阶段可以使用SetCare代替标准孵化器进一步优化。在SetCare中，鸡蛋在储存后以每小时0.1华氏度的受控速率逐渐升温。这种在胚胎细胞复制初期（从85华氏度到100华氏度）的缓慢升温，可显著降低死亡率，并产生更强壮、更均匀的雏鸡。”

Wijnen继续说道：“在第三阶段，研究表明，使用HatchCare可以在雏鸡出壳后立即提供饲料、水和光照，从而改善家禽生产性能。”

**我看到三个相当创新的系统和一个挑战传统孵化实践的概念组合。你们疯了吗？**

“在我们公司成立的头10-15年里，行业中的许多人……”
author: Fabian Brockötter
